facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Vale household lane in Brackenside’s lower debt quarter, after moving from the guild annex healing ward through the annex yard and old streets toward home

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Unranked, unlicensed salvage runner; awake but battered, shoulder stiff, briefly dizzy on standing, under growing legal scrutiny after the South Third escape; Residual Hearing remains ungraded with a retained provisional record now apparently subject to hall review; emotionally resolved to speak plainly about the missing men and fight the paper battle openly

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Attend the hall board at second bell, secure witness support for his emergency-recovery judgment, reduce or survive the qualification fee/bond barrier, and keep Pike/guild interests from burying the old corridor or the missing-men issue

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Rowan still lacks money for the filing fee, lacks a sponsor, and has no institutional protection; Pike-linked agents are collecting and comparing statements; Jeren is inaccessible under treatment, Toma is incapacitated, Mett is absent, Benn and Derren remain missing, and Rowan now faces family anger and public blame as well as procedural risk to his retained notice

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Lio is an active strategic ally and reveals he is provisional himself; Sena agrees to testify truthfully in person and may attend the board if able; Benn’s sister is not an ally to Rowan personally but is aligned against guild minimization of the missing-men case; Jeren remains a potential witness but is presently unavailable; Elias is present and emotionally volatile rather than aligned in-scene

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: The struggle has fully shifted into legal-economic and social territory: Rowan must survive a same-day board review, secure enough testimony to defend his actions and claim relevance, and navigate hostility from Pike’s side, institutional process, grieving families, and his father while still lacking the money and paper needed to become countable on the guild’s terms
